R Notebook

data_complete<- read.csv("R_Practice/20250919_1143_SporeLawnvVegLawn.csv")

data <- data_complete
## turn data that is TNTC or 0 (can't be used to calculate pfu/cfu) into NA
data <- subset(data, data$count!="TNTC" & data$count!="WO" & data$count!="0")

## turn Count column into "numeric" category of data so that R recognizes it is numbers, not words.
data$count <- as.numeric(data$count)

#PFU/mL = PFUs/Innoc uL * 1000uL/1mL * DilutionFactor  (calculate PFU/CFU of viruses/spores)
##make it log10, because that is better for graphing 10^X
data$norm <- log10((data$count)/200 * 1000 * 10^data$dilution)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(data, aes(x = factor(lawn), fill = factor(lawn), y = norm )) + geom_boxplot()




ggplot(data, aes(x = factor(lawn), fill = factor(lawn), y = norm )) +
  geom_boxplot(binaxis = "y", stackdir = "center", position = "dodge", color = "black") + ylab("log10 Plates per mL") + ggtitle("Plaques per Lawn") + xlab("Lawn Typw")  +  geom_jitter(
    aes(fill = factor(lawn)),  shape = 21, color = "black", width = 0.2, size = 2) + 
scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 9), breaks = seq(0, 9, 1))+ 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `binaxis` and `stackdir`


## null model
a.null <- aov(norm ~ 1, data = data)
summary(a.null)
            Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
Residuals    7  1.402  0.2003               
## basic anova of just does lawn change the number of counts
a1 <- aov(norm ~ lawn,
  data = data)
summary(a1)
            Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
lawn         2 0.4666  0.2333   1.247  0.364
Residuals    5 0.9356  0.1871               
## check which model is best, lowest is best!
AIC(a.null, a1)
NA
NA
## what if OD doesn't matter


ggplot(data, aes(x = factor(type), fill = factor(type), y = norm )) +
  geom_boxplot(binaxis = "y", stackdir = "center", position = "dodge", color = "black") + ylab("log10 Plates per mL") + ggtitle("Plaques per Lawn") + xlab("Lawn Typw")  +  geom_jitter(
    aes(fill = factor(type)),  shape = 21, color = "black", width = 0.2, size = 2) + 
scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 9), breaks = seq(0, 9, 1))+ 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `binaxis` and `stackdir`

## basic anova of just does lawn change the number of counts
a1 <- aov(norm ~ type,
  data = data)
summary(a1)
            Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
type         1 0.4383  0.4383   2.729   0.15
Residuals    6 0.9639  0.1606               
## check which model is best, lowest is best!
AIC(a.null, a1)
NA
NA
NA
NA
